events.note: Password spray: same password across multiple accounts from one IP (SubStatus 0xC000006A = wrong password)
Spray against second account — same IP, different user (key spray indicator)
Kerberos pre-auth failure — attacker also spraying via Kerberos path
Successful logon after spray — credential found

mutation_hints.objective: Obtain valid credentials for lateral movement via password spray
detection_anchors: Global threshold: stats count as total_failures by _time (5-min bucket) where total_failures >= 12 → reduce count per spec below 12/2=6 (2 specs) so total events in window stays under 12; or use spread_seconds > 300 to push events into separate 5-min buckets

Spray-IPs indicator: stats dc(Source_Network_Address) as spray_ips where spray_ips >= 2 → if all events come from ONE IP (spray_ips=1) this branch does NOT fire; rotate Source_Network_Address across specs or use a single fixed IP for all events
Spray-accounts indicator: dc(Account_Name) per window; using many distinct Account_Name values across the spray events is still a secondary signal; reduce variety of target accounts
Field match: Sub_Status=0xC000006A (wrong password) → use 0xC000006D for general logon failure or 0xC0000064 for unknown username — still a failure but different sub-status
Field match: Authentication_Package=NTLM → switch to Negotiate; legitimate clients mostly use Negotiate which is less alarming than explicit NTLM
Field match: Logon_Type=3 (Network) → use Logon_Type=8 (NetworkCleartext) for plaintext-auth spray variant, or Logon_Type=2 to simulate interactive
Field match: Logon_Process=NtLmSsp → use Kerberos or User32 to alter the auth path fingerprint
